title: Lazy Sunday Moroccan Boudin Sellou
description: A delicious and easy-to-make Moroccan boudin sellou that's perfect for a lazy Sunday breakfast or brunch.

ingredients.name: Boudin Noir
ingredients.notes: High-quality boudin noir is essential for this recipe.

ingredients.name: Lard or vegetable shortening
ingredients.notes: Use lard for a more authentic flavor or vegetable shortening as a substitute.

ingredients.name: Granulated sugar
ingredients.notes: Use pure cane sugar for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: Egg
ingredients.notes: Large eggs are best for this recipe.

ingredients.name: Unsalted butter
ingredients.notes: Softened unsalted butter makes the best sellou.

ingredients.name: Almonds
ingredients.notes: Sliced or slivered almonds add a nice crunch.

ingredients.name: Confectioners' sugar
ingredients.notes: Dust the sellou with confectioners' sugar before serving.

instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour, granulated sugar, and a pinch of salt.
Add the lard or shortening and use a pastry blender or your fingers to work it into the dry ingredients until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
Add the eggs and mix until the dough comes together in a ball.
Wrap the dough in plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes.
On a lightly floured surface, roll out the dough to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
Cut out circles of dough using a cookie cutter or the rim of a glass.
Fry the dough circles in hot oil (about 375°F or 190°C) until they are golden brown and puffed up.
Drain the sellou on paper towels and let cool slightly.
Dust the sellou with confectioners' sugar before serving.
